title: Initialize Next.js project with TypeScript and App Router
description: Create the Next.js project scaffold using create-next-app with TypeScript, App Router, and src/ directory structure. Configure tsconfig.json with strict mode, path aliases (@/), and moduleResolution bundler.
acceptanceCriteria: Next.js app boots with `next dev` without errors
TypeScript strict mode enabled in tsconfig.json
Path alias @/ resolves to src/ directory

title: Install core dependencies (React Flow, Zustand, motion/react, shadcn)
description: Install and configure all primary runtime dependencies: @xyflow/react for the canvas, zustand for state management, motion/react for animations, and shadcn/ui CLI for component generation. Pin versions to prevent drift.
acceptanceCriteria: All packages listed in package.json with pinned versions
shadcn components.json configured with correct aliases and style
No version conflicts in dependency tree

title: Configure Tailwind CSS v4 with design tokens
description: Set up Tailwind CSS v4 with @theme inline directive, CSS custom properties for the Architex design system, and dark/light mode support. Include 4-layer background system, violet primary accent, semantic state colors, node type colors, and visualization chart palette.
acceptanceCriteria: globals.css contains all design tokens for dark and light themes
@theme inline block maps CSS vars to Tailwind utilities
Reduced motion media query overrides all animation durations

title: Set up React Flow canvas with custom node types
description: Create DesignCanvas component with ReactFlow provider, custom node type registry (WebServer, LoadBalancer, Database, Cache, MessageQueue, APIGateway, CDN, Client, Storage), custom DataFlowEdge, background dots, minimap, and controls panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Canvas renders with dot grid background using --canvas-bg and --canvas-dot tokens
All 9 system-design node types registered in nodeTypes map
Nodes are draggable, selectable, and connectable via handles

title: Create Zustand stores (canvas, simulation, UI, viewport, editor, interview)
description: Implement all 6 Zustand stores: canvas-store (nodes, edges, CRUD operations), simulation-store (status, metrics, traffic config, chaos events), ui-store (activeModule, theme, panel visibility), viewport-store (zoom, pan, fitView), editor-store (code, language), interview-store (challenges, timer, scoring).
acceptanceCriteria: All 6 stores export typed hooks (useCanvasStore, useSimulationStore, etc.)
Stores barrel-exported from src/stores/index.ts
Canvas store supports addNode, removeNode, updateNodeData, addEdge, removeEdge

title: Implement theme provider with dark/light/system toggle
description: Create ThemeProvider using next-themes wrapping the app layout. Support dark, light, and system preference modes. Persist theme choice to localStorage. Apply theme class to html element for CSS variable switching.
acceptanceCriteria: ThemeProvider wraps app in layout.tsx with attribute='class'
Theme toggle cycles dark -> light -> system
CSS variables in globals.css switch correctly between :root/.dark and html.light

title: Build command palette with fuzzy search
description: Implement VS Code-style command palette (Cmd+K) with fuzzy search across all available actions. Categories include canvas operations, simulation controls, module switching, export, and undo/redo. Keyboard navigable with Enter to execute.
acceptanceCriteria: Cmd+K / Ctrl+K opens command palette dialog
Fuzzy search filters commands by title and category
Arrow keys + Enter navigate and execute commands

title: Create activity bar with module switching icons
description: Build the left-edge vertical activity bar (VS Code style) with icon buttons for each module: System Design, Algorithms, Data Structures, LLD, Database, Distributed Systems, Networking, OS, Concurrency, Security, ML Design, Interview. Highlight active module with primary accent.
acceptanceCriteria: Activity bar renders at 48px width on left edge
Clicking an icon switches activeModule in ui-store
Active module icon highlighted with --primary background

title: Create status bar with simulation state and node count
description: Build the bottom status bar displaying: active module name, simulation status (idle/running/paused), node count, edge count, current zoom level, and keyboard shortcut hints. Uses --statusbar and --statusbar-foreground tokens.
acceptanceCriteria: Status bar renders at 24px height at bottom edge
Displays live node/edge counts from canvas-store
Simulation status badge updates reactively from simulation-store

title: Register keyboard shortcuts (undo, redo, delete, save, zoom)
description: Create useKeyboardShortcuts hook registering global keyboard bindings: Cmd+Z (undo), Cmd+Shift+Z (redo), Delete/Backspace (remove selected), Cmd+S (save), Cmd+= / Cmd+- (zoom), Cmd+0 (fit view), Cmd+K (command palette), Escape (deselect). Prevent default browser behavior for all bound keys.
acceptanceCriteria: All shortcuts fire correct store actions
Shortcuts are suppressed when input/textarea is focused
Hook cleans up event listeners on unmount

title: Set up Clerk authentication with sign-in and sign-up pages
description: Install @clerk/nextjs, wrap app layout in ClerkProvider, create /sign-in/[[...sign-in]] and /sign-up/[[...sign-up]] catch-all routes using Clerk's pre-built components. Configure publishableKey and secretKey from environment variables. Add middleware.ts to protect routes.
acceptanceCriteria: ClerkProvider wraps entire app in layout.tsx
/sign-in and /sign-up routes render Clerk UI components
middleware.ts uses clerkMiddleware() to protect /dashboard and /api routes
NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY and CLERK_SECRET_KEY documented in .env.example

title: Create requireAuth() server boundary utility (CVE-2025-29927)
description: Implement a requireAuth() utility that calls auth() from @clerk/nextjs/server at every server component and API route boundary. This guards against the middleware-bypass vulnerability (CVE-2025-29927) where x-middleware-subrequest headers can skip middleware auth checks. Every Server Component and Route Handler that accesses user data must call requireAuth() as the first line.
acceptanceCriteria: requireAuth() exported from src/lib/auth.ts
Function throws redirect to /sign-in if auth().userId is null
All existing server components and API routes call requireAuth()
Unit test verifies redirect behavior on unauthenticated request

title: Configure Neon database connection with HTTP, WebSocket, and TCP modes
description: Set up @neondatabase/serverless with three connection modes: HTTP (for serverless functions via neon()), WebSocket (for edge runtime via Pool with neonConfig.webSocketConstructor), and TCP (for local dev via pg Pool). Export a unified db object from src/lib/db.ts that selects the correct mode based on runtime environment.
acceptanceCriteria: DATABASE_URL env var used for all connection modes
HTTP mode works in API route handlers (serverless)
WebSocket mode works in edge middleware
Health check query SELECT 1 succeeds in all modes

title: Run Drizzle ORM schema generation and initial migration
description: Configure drizzle-kit with drizzle.config.ts pointing to Neon DATABASE_URL. Define initial schema tables (users, projects, diagrams) in src/lib/db/schema.ts using Drizzle's pgTable. Run `drizzle-kit generate` to create SQL migration files and `drizzle-kit migrate` to apply them to Neon database.
acceptanceCriteria: drizzle.config.ts configured with dialect 'postgresql' and schema path
At least users and projects tables defined in schema.ts
drizzle/ directory contains generated migration SQL files
Migration applies cleanly to Neon database

title: Set up Inngest for background job processing
description: Install inngest package, create Inngest client in src/lib/inngest/client.ts with app ID 'architex'. Create the serve route at src/app/api/inngest/route.ts using the serve() handler. Define one example function (e.g., diagram.exported event handler) to validate the pipeline works end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: Inngest client exported from src/lib/inngest/client.ts
API route at /api/inngest serves the Inngest handler
npx inngest-cli dev shows the registered function
Example function triggers and completes on event send

title: Set up Resend email client with first transactional template
description: Install resend package, create email client in src/lib/email/client.ts with RESEND_API_KEY from env. Create first React Email template (WelcomeEmail) using @react-email/components. Add a sendWelcomeEmail() function that renders the template and sends via Resend API.
acceptanceCriteria: Resend client instantiated with API key from env
WelcomeEmail template renders with user name and CTA button
sendWelcomeEmail() sends to specified address and returns message ID
RESEND_API_KEY documented in .env.example

title: Set up PostHog analytics with GDPR consent banner
description: Install posthog-js, create PostHogProvider that initializes the client with NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY. Implement a GDPR-compliant cookie consent banner that calls posthog.opt_in_capturing() or posthog.opt_out_capturing() based on user choice. Persist consent state to localStorage. Only load PostHog script after opt-in.
acceptanceCriteria: PostHog initializes only after user consents to analytics
Consent banner appears on first visit with Accept/Decline buttons
Declining analytics sets posthog.opt_out_capturing()
Page views and custom events tracked after opt-in

title: Set up Sentry error tracking for client, server, and edge runtimes
description: Install @sentry/nextjs, run the Sentry wizard to generate sentry.client.config.ts, sentry.server.config.ts, and sentry.edge.config.ts. Configure DSN from SENTRY_DSN env var. Set tracesSampleRate to 0.1 for production, 1.0 for development. Add source map upload to next.config.ts via withSentryConfig wrapper.
acceptanceCriteria: Client-side errors reported to Sentry dashboard
Server-side errors in API routes reported to Sentry
Source maps uploaded for readable stack traces
SENTRY_DSN and SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN documented in .env.example

title: Define ArchitexNode canonical type with all data fields
description: Create the ArchitexNode interface in src/lib/canvas/types.ts as the domain-level node representation. Includes id, type, position, and ArchitexNodeData (label, category, componentType, icon, config, metrics, state). This type is React Flow-agnostic and serves as the source of truth for serialization, persistence, and export.
acceptanceCriteria: ArchitexNode interface exported from src/lib/canvas/types.ts
ArchitexNodeData includes all fields: label, category, componentType, icon, config, metrics, state
NodeCategory and NodeState union types cover all valid values
JSDoc comments explain each field's purpose

title: Define ArchitexEdge canonical type with protocol and metrics
description: Create the ArchitexEdge interface in src/lib/canvas/types.ts as the domain-level edge representation. Includes id, source, target, sourceHandle, targetHandle, and ArchitexEdgeData (edgeType covering http/grpc/graphql/websocket/message-queue/event-stream/db-query/cache-lookup/replication, plus latency, bandwidth, errorRate, animated fields).
acceptanceCriteria: ArchitexEdge interface exported from src/lib/canvas/types.ts
EdgeType union covers all 9 protocol types
All metric fields (latency, bandwidth, errorRate) are optional numbers
Type is distinct from @xyflow/react Edge type

title: Implement toReactFlowNode and toReactFlowEdge converters
description: Create forward converters in src/lib/canvas/adapter.ts that transform ArchitexNode -> @xyflow/react Node and ArchitexEdge -> @xyflow/react Edge. Deep-copy position to prevent shared reference mutations. Map ArchitexEdge.data.edgeType to RF's type field ('data-flow' custom edge).
acceptanceCriteria: toReactFlowNode() returns a valid RF Node with deep-copied position
toReactFlowEdge() returns RF Edge with type='data-flow'
Batch converters toReactFlowNodes() and toReactFlowEdges() exported
Unit tests verify round-trip data integrity

title: Implement fromReactFlowNode and fromReactFlowEdge converters
description: Create reverse converters in src/lib/canvas/adapter.ts that transform @xyflow/react Node -> ArchitexNode and Edge -> ArchitexEdge. Strip RF internal properties (measured, selected, dragging, positionAbsolute, width, height). Apply safe defaults for missing fields (type defaults to 'default', state defaults to 'idle', category defaults to 'compute').
acceptanceCriteria: fromReactFlowNode() strips all RF rendering internals
Missing rfNode.type defaults to 'default'
fromReactFlowEdge() coerces null sourceHandle/targetHandle to undefined
Edge cases: empty data object, null fields all produce valid ArchitexNode/Edge

title: Integrate adapter into canvas-store for internal ArchitexNode storage
description: Refactor canvas-store to internally store ArchitexNode[] and ArchitexEdge[] instead of RF Node[] and Edge[]. Expose derived RF nodes/edges via a selector that calls toReactFlowNodes/Edges. Update addNode, removeNode, updateNodeData, addEdge, removeEdge to operate on canonical types.
acceptanceCriteria: canvas-store internal state uses ArchitexNode[] and ArchitexEdge[]
useCanvasStore.getState().nodes returns ArchitexNode[]
New selector useReactFlowNodes() returns RF Node[] for DesignCanvas
All CRUD operations validate against ArchitexNode type

title: Update DesignCanvas to consume adapter selectors
description: Modify DesignCanvas.tsx to use the new useReactFlowNodes() and useReactFlowEdges() selectors instead of directly reading RF nodes from canvas-store. Update onDrop to create ArchitexNode first, then let the store convert. Update onConnect to create ArchitexEdge.
acceptanceCriteria: DesignCanvas renders nodes from useReactFlowNodes() selector
onDrop creates ArchitexNode with crypto.randomUUID() for ID
onConnect creates ArchitexEdge with correct sourceHandle/targetHandle
No direct import of ArchitexNode in DesignCanvas (only RF types)

title: Update all export functions to consume ArchitexNode/Edge types
description: Refactor to-mermaid.ts, to-plantuml.ts, to-json.ts, to-terraform.ts, and to-url.ts to accept ArchitexNode[] and ArchitexEdge[] instead of RF Node[] and Edge[]. This removes the @xyflow/react dependency from the entire export subsystem.
acceptanceCriteria: All 5 export functions accept ArchitexNode[] and ArchitexEdge[] parameters
No import from @xyflow/react in any src/lib/export/ file
Export outputs are identical before and after refactor
to-json.ts exports ArchitexNode/Edge directly (clean serialization)

title: Create Command Bus dispatcher with typed handler registry
description: Implement createCommandBus() in src/stores/command-bus.ts with a synchronous dispatch function, a typed handler map keyed by Command['type'], command audit log (last 200 entries), and subscriber pattern for devtools. Returns CommandResult with success/error/data. NOT an event emitter and NOT Redux middleware.
acceptanceCriteria: commandBus.dispatch() synchronously executes the correct handler
CommandResult returned with success boolean and error code
Audit log stores last 200 entries with timestamps and duration
subscribe() callback fires after every dispatch

title: Implement LOAD_TEMPLATE command handler
description: Wire the LOAD_TEMPLATE command handler in the command bus. Handler resolves template by ID, snapshots current state for undo via beginTransaction(), converts template nodes/edges to ArchitexNodes via adapter, sets canvas-store nodes/edges (append or replace mode), resets simulation-store, sets ui-store to system-design module, and commits undo transaction.
acceptanceCriteria: LOAD_TEMPLATE with valid templateId replaces canvas nodes/edges
LOAD_TEMPLATE with append=true appends instead of replacing
Unknown templateId returns { success: false, error: 'TEMPLATE_NOT_FOUND' }
Undo after LOAD_TEMPLATE restores previous canvas state

title: Implement START_SIMULATION and STOP_SIMULATION command handlers
description: Wire simulation lifecycle commands. START_SIMULATION optionally applies trafficConfig to simulation-store, sets status to 'running', and updates all canvas node states to 'active'. STOP_SIMULATION sets status to 'stopped', resets all node states to 'idle', and clears runtime metrics.
acceptanceCriteria: START_SIMULATION sets simulation-store status to 'running'
All canvas nodes transition to state='active' on start
STOP_SIMULATION resets all nodes to state='idle' with empty metrics
Optional trafficConfig payload overrides simulation-store config

title: Implement INJECT_CHAOS and REMOVE_CHAOS command handlers
description: Wire chaos engineering commands. INJECT_CHAOS adds a chaos event to simulation-store by type, marks targeted node IDs as state='error' in canvas-store. REMOVE_CHAOS removes the chaos event and reverts affected nodes to state='active' if simulation is still running.
acceptanceCriteria: INJECT_CHAOS marks specified nodes as state='error'
REMOVE_CHAOS reverts error nodes to 'active' when simulation running
REMOVE_CHAOS leaves nodes as 'idle' when simulation is stopped
Multiple chaos types can be active simultaneously

title: Implement EXPORT, LOAD_PROJECT, RESET_WORKSPACE, and SWITCH_MODULE handlers
description: Wire the remaining 4 command handlers. EXPORT delegates to the existing export functions by format, returning output as data. LOAD_PROJECT hydrates all stores from SerializedProject within an undo transaction. RESET_WORKSPACE clears canvas, simulation, editor, and interview stores atomically. SWITCH_MODULE updates ui-store.activeModule with optional canvas preservation.
acceptanceCriteria: EXPORT returns serialized output for all 5 formats (mermaid, plantuml, json, terraform, url)
LOAD_PROJECT restores full workspace state from SerializedProject
RESET_WORKSPACE clears all stores within a single undo transaction
SWITCH_MODULE changes active module and optionally preserves canvas

title: Create useCommandBus React hook and integrate with command palette
description: Export useCommandBus() hook that returns the singleton commandBus instance. Refactor command palette actions to dispatch commands through the bus instead of calling stores directly. Wire the 8 currently no-op 'Add Component' actions to dispatch appropriate commands.
acceptanceCriteria: useCommandBus() returns commandBus singleton
Command palette uses commandBus.dispatch() for all actions
All command palette items produce valid CommandLogEntry in audit log
DevTools can subscribe to command stream via commandBus.subscribe()

title: Create UndoManager with snapshot-based undo/redo stacks
description: Implement createUndoManager() in src/stores/undo-manager.ts to replace zundo. Uses UndoSnapshot capturing state from canvas-store (as ArchitexNode/Edge), simulation-store (trafficConfig), ui-store (activeModule), and editor-store (code, language). Undo/redo operates by applying full snapshots across all stores simultaneously.
acceptanceCriteria: undoManager.pushSnapshot(label) captures cross-store state
undoManager.undo() restores previous snapshot across all stores
undoManager.redo() moves forward in history
canUndo() and canRedo() return correct booleans

title: Add transaction API to UndoManager (beginTransaction / commitTransaction)
description: Extend UndoManager with transaction support. beginTransaction() captures a pre-change snapshot. Multiple store mutations between begin and commit produce exactly ONE undo entry. commitTransaction(label) pushes the pre-transaction snapshot to the undo stack. rollbackTransaction() restores to pre-transaction state without creating an entry.
acceptanceCriteria: beginTransaction() + commitTransaction() creates exactly one undo entry
pushSnapshot() is suppressed while a transaction is open
rollbackTransaction() restores state without adding to undo stack
Nested beginTransaction() calls are rejected (returns without action)

title: Implement drag debounce for undo via onNodeDragStart/Stop
description: Wire DesignCanvas onNodeDragStart to call undoManager.beginTransaction() and onNodeDragStop to call undoManager.commitTransaction('Move node(s)'). This ensures that a full drag gesture (which fires onNodesChange 60x/sec) produces exactly ONE undo entry instead of 60+ entries per second.
acceptanceCriteria: Dragging a node from position A to B creates exactly 1 undo entry
Undo after drag restores node to original position
Multi-node drag (selection) also produces 1 undo entry
Rapid sequential drags each produce their own entry

title: Add memory cap and entry limit to undo stack
description: Enforce two simultaneous limits on the undo stack: MAX_ENTRIES=100 (hard count) and MAX_SIZE_BYTES=10MB (memory pressure). Size estimation uses JSON.stringify(snapshot).length * 2 for UTF-16 overhead. When either limit is exceeded, oldest entries are evicted. Redo stack is cleared on any new push.
acceptanceCriteria: Undo stack never exceeds 100 entries
Undo stack total memory never exceeds 10MB estimated
Oldest entries evicted first when limits are hit
getStackInfo() reports current undoSize and redoSize

title: Remove zundo dependency and migrate existing undo logic
description: Remove the zundo package from dependencies. Delete all zundo middleware usage in canvas-store (temporal/partialize). Update all undo/redo call sites (keyboard shortcuts, command palette) to use undoManager.undo() and undoManager.redo() instead of zundo's useTemporalStore.undo()/redo().
acceptanceCriteria: zundo no longer in package.json or any import statements
Cmd+Z calls undoManager.undo()
Cmd+Shift+Z calls undoManager.redo()
No regression in undo/redo behavior for add/remove/move operations

title: Add 500ms debounced pushSnapshot for rapid config changes
description: For non-drag operations that fire rapidly (typing in config panel, adjusting sliders), wrap undoManager.pushSnapshot() in a 500ms debounce. Create a debouncedPush utility that groups rapid changes into a single undo entry. Use in PropertiesPanel and any config-editing UI.
acceptanceCriteria: Typing 10 characters in config panel produces at most 1-2 undo entries
Debounce window is 500ms of inactivity
Non-debounced pushSnapshot still works for discrete actions (add node, delete)
Slider adjustments debounced to single undo entry per drag

title: Create useUndoRedo React hook with reactive stack info
description: Export a useUndoRedo() hook that exposes undo(), redo(), canUndo, canRedo, and stackInfo (undoSize, redoSize). Uses useSyncExternalStore or a Zustand-backed reactive wrapper so that canUndo/canRedo update UI (undo/redo button disabled states) reactively.
acceptanceCriteria: useUndoRedo() returns typed object with all undo/redo operations
canUndo/canRedo update reactively when stack changes
Undo/redo buttons in toolbar disable correctly based on canUndo/canRedo
stackInfo shows current entry counts for devtools display

title: Set up IndexedDB via Dexie for project persistence
description: Install dexie package and create the 'architex-db' database in src/lib/persistence/db.ts. Define three object stores: 'projects' (keyed by project.id), 'autosave' (keyed by 'current'), and 'preferences' (keyed by 'ui'). Use Dexie's versioned schema for future migrations.
acceptanceCriteria: Dexie database 'architex-db' opens successfully in browser
Three object stores created: projects, autosave, preferences
CRUD operations (put, get, delete) work on all stores
Database version set to 1 with migration hooks ready

title: Implement auto-save with dirty flag and 2-second debounce
description: Create Zustand middleware (dirtyMiddleware) that sets a dirty flag on any store mutation. After dirty=true, schedule an IndexedDB save after 2000ms of inactivity. Also trigger immediate save on window 'blur' event (tab switch). The auto-save captures SerializedProject from all stores using the adapter to produce ArchitexNode/Edge.
acceptanceCriteria: Adding/moving/deleting a node triggers auto-save after 2s
Rapid mutations (dragging) coalesce into single save
Switching browser tabs triggers immediate save
Save writes SerializedProject to IndexedDB 'autosave' store with key 'current'

title: Implement hydration pipeline with ordered store loading
description: Create hydration pipeline in src/lib/persistence/hydration.ts that loads stores in correct order on app startup: (1) ui-store from localStorage (sync, already done), (2) canvas-store + editor-store from IndexedDB autosave (async, with loading state), (3) simulation-store starts fresh, (4) viewport-store calls fitView after canvas loads. Expose HydrationState Zustand store with uiReady, canvasReady, fullyReady flags.
acceptanceCriteria: UI renders immediately with theme from localStorage
Canvas skeleton shown while IndexedDB loads (canvasReady=false)
Canvas populated with nodes/edges from last autosave on load
fullyReady=true only after all stores hydrated

title: Implement schema migration chain for SerializedProject versioning
description: Create a migration registry in src/lib/persistence/migrations.ts. Each migration is a function that transforms SerializedProject from version N to N+1. On hydration, if the stored version is behind SCHEMA_VERSION, run the migration chain sequentially. Start with version 1 as the baseline; add migration stubs for version 2 as a template.
acceptanceCriteria: migrateProject() runs all required migrations in sequence
Missing migration throws descriptive error
Version 1 data loads without migration
Migration template for v1->v2 demonstrates the pattern

title: Add beforeunload fallback save to localStorage
description: Register a window 'beforeunload' event that synchronously saves a JSON snapshot to localStorage as a fallback (IndexedDB writes are async and may not complete before page unload). On next load, hydration checks localStorage fallback first, then IndexedDB. Fallback is deleted after successful IndexedDB hydration.
acceptanceCriteria: Closing the browser tab triggers synchronous localStorage save
Fallback data is restored if IndexedDB save failed before unload
Fallback is cleaned up after successful IndexedDB hydration
localStorage key is 'architex-fallback-save'

title: Create ModuleRenderer component with React.lazy dynamic imports
description: Replace the current useModuleContent() hook pattern with a ModuleRenderer component that uses a switch statement and React.lazy() for each module. Only the active module's component is rendered; inactive modules are unmounted, releasing all useState, useEffect, and useRef resources. System-design module is always eagerly loaded.
acceptanceCriteria: Only active module component is mounted in the React tree
Switching modules unmounts the previous module (verified via useEffect cleanup)
System-design module loads eagerly (no dynamic import)
All other modules use React.lazy(() => import(...))

title: Wrap ModuleRenderer in Suspense with ModuleSkeleton fallback
description: Create a ModuleSkeleton component that mimics the module layout (sidebar placeholder, canvas area, bottom panel) using skeleton pulse animations. Wrap ModuleRenderer in React.Suspense with ModuleSkeleton as the fallback. Skeleton uses the --surface and --elevated tokens for placeholder blocks.
acceptanceCriteria: Suspense boundary wraps ModuleRenderer
ModuleSkeleton renders during dynamic import load
Skeleton layout matches actual module layout proportions
Skeleton uses --surface background with pulse animation

title: Implement warm-resume for interview module via IndexedDB
description: When InterviewModule unmounts, save current state (challengeId, timerSeconds, hintsUsed, canvas snapshot) to IndexedDB. On remount, check for saved state and restore it instead of starting fresh. This prevents interview progress loss when switching modules.
acceptanceCriteria: Switching away from interview module saves progress to IndexedDB
Switching back to interview module restores timer, challenge, and hints
Starting a new interview clears the saved warm-resume state
Cold start (no saved state) initializes interview normally

title: Remove all unconditional module hooks from page.tsx
description: Delete the 7 useXxxModule() hook calls that currently run on every render regardless of active module. Replace with the ModuleRenderer component-based approach. Ensure no module-specific hooks remain in the page-level component.
acceptanceCriteria: No useAlgorithmModule, useDistributedModule, etc. calls in page.tsx
Active module determined solely by ModuleRenderer switch statement
Bundle size for initial page load reduced (verified via next build output)
No regression in any module's functionality

title: Add module-scoped context providers for complex module state
description: For modules with complex internal state that would require prop drilling (AlgorithmModule, InterviewModule), create scoped React Context providers that are created and destroyed with the module component. Use useReducer within the provider for predictable state updates.
acceptanceCriteria: AlgorithmContext.Provider wraps AlgorithmModule subtree
Context is null-checked in useAlgorithmContext() hook
Provider is destroyed when module unmounts (no stale state)
Pattern documented as a template for future modules

title: Create app-level error.tsx and global-error.tsx boundaries
description: Create src/app/error.tsx as a client component error boundary that catches runtime errors in the app. Shows a styled error card with the error message, a 'Try Again' button (calls reset()), and a 'Go Home' link. Create src/app/global-error.tsx as the root-level fallback that catches errors in layout.tsx itself, rendering a minimal HTML page without any providers.
acceptanceCriteria: error.tsx catches and displays errors from page components
reset() button retries the failed render
global-error.tsx renders without ThemeProvider or any context
Error details are logged to console (and Sentry when configured)

title: Create app-level loading.tsx with full-page skeleton
description: Create src/app/loading.tsx that renders a full-page skeleton matching the workspace layout: activity bar placeholder, sidebar placeholder, main canvas area with centered spinner/pulse, bottom panel placeholder, and status bar placeholder. Uses Tailwind animate-pulse and design tokens.
acceptanceCriteria: loading.tsx renders during page transitions and initial load
Skeleton proportions match actual workspace layout
Uses --background, --surface, --elevated tokens for layers
Reduced motion users see static placeholders (no pulse)

title: Add 12-step gray scale tokens for dark and light themes
description: Extend globals.css with a full 12-step gray scale (gray-1 through gray-12) based on the existing HSL 228 15% base hue. Dark theme ranges from 7% to 90% lightness. Light theme ranges from 99% to 10% lightness. These provide fine-grained control for backgrounds, borders, text, and overlays beyond the current 4-layer system.
acceptanceCriteria: --gray-1 through --gray-12 defined for both :root/.dark and html.light
Gray scale follows consistent HSL progression with matching hue/saturation
Mapped to Tailwind via @theme inline as --color-gray-1 through --color-gray-12
Existing tokens (--background, --surface, --elevated, --overlay) remain unchanged

title: Add full violet accent scale (12 steps) for primary color system
description: Extend the primary violet from the current 3 tokens (--primary, --primary-foreground, --primary-hover) to a full 12-step scale (--violet-1 through --violet-12). Dark theme: subtle tinted backgrounds at low end, full saturation at mid, bright at high. Light theme: inverted lightness curve. Enables gradient fills, hover states, focus rings, and selection highlights.
acceptanceCriteria: --violet-1 through --violet-12 defined for both themes
Existing --primary maps to approximately --violet-9
--violet-1 and --violet-2 are subtle enough for background tints
All 12 steps pass WCAG contrast requirements against their typical use case

title: Add semantic sub-tokens for success state (background, text, border)
description: Break --state-success into three sub-tokens: --success-bg (subtle background fill), --success-text (readable text on both bg and surface), --success-border (visible border). Dark theme: bg ~15% lightness with green tint, text ~65% lightness, border ~30% lightness. Light theme: bg ~95%, text ~30%, border ~75%.
acceptanceCriteria: --success-bg, --success-text, --success-border defined for both themes
Text passes WCAG AA contrast on both --success-bg and --surface
Mapped to Tailwind utilities via @theme inline
Used in at least one component (toast, badge, or status indicator)

title: Add semantic sub-tokens for warning state (background, text, border)
description: Break --state-warning into three sub-tokens: --warning-bg, --warning-text, --warning-border. Follow the same pattern as success tokens with amber/orange hue. Ensure warning text is readable on both warning-bg and default surface backgrounds.
acceptanceCriteria: --warning-bg, --warning-text, --warning-border defined for both themes
Text passes WCAG AA contrast on both --warning-bg and --surface
Hue consistent with existing --state-warning (HSL 38)
Mapped to Tailwind utilities via @theme inline

title: Add semantic sub-tokens for error state (background, text, border)
description: Break --state-error and --destructive into three sub-tokens: --error-bg, --error-text, --error-border. Red hue (HSL 0) with appropriate lightness for each role. These are distinct from --destructive-* which are for buttons; error tokens are for status displays, alerts, and validation messages.
acceptanceCriteria: --error-bg, --error-text, --error-border defined for both themes
Distinct from --destructive (buttons) — error is for status/feedback
Text passes WCAG AA contrast on both --error-bg and --surface
Mapped to Tailwind utilities via @theme inline

title: Add semantic sub-tokens for info state (background, text, border)
description: Add --info-bg, --info-text, --info-border tokens using blue hue (HSL ~217, matching --state-active). These are for informational alerts, tooltips with context, and info badges. Not currently in the token system but needed for complete semantic coverage.
acceptanceCriteria: --info-bg, --info-text, --info-border defined for both themes
Blue hue consistent with --state-active (HSL 217)
Text passes WCAG AA contrast on both --info-bg and --surface
Mapped to Tailwind utilities via @theme inline

title: Add border variant tokens (subtle, default, strong, focus)
description: Extend the single --border token into four variants: --border-subtle (barely visible separators), --border (current default), --border-strong (emphasized divisions), --border-focus (keyboard focus rings, matching --ring). This allows components to use appropriate border emphasis levels.
acceptanceCriteria: --border-subtle, --border, --border-strong, --border-focus defined for both themes
Existing --border value unchanged (backward compatible)
--border-focus matches current --ring value
All four variants mapped to Tailwind via @theme inline

title: Add shadow system tokens (sm, md, lg, xl, inner)
description: Add CSS custom property shadow tokens: --shadow-sm (subtle lift), --shadow-md (card elevation), --shadow-lg (dropdown/popover), --shadow-xl (modal/dialog), --shadow-inner (inset for inputs). Dark theme uses darker, more diffuse shadows. Light theme uses lighter, crisper shadows. All shadows use neutral colors, no colored shadows.

title: Define 9-step font size scale with CSS tokens

title: Add shadcn/ui ScrollArea, Tabs, and Switch components

title: Create ToolbarButton custom component

Tooltip shows on hover after 200ms delay

description: Build a SidebarItem component for use in the component palette, file tree, and other sidebar lists. Features: icon + label + optional badge/count, hover highlight with --elevated, selected state with --primary tint, indentation support for nested items, drag handle for palette items.

Hover highlights with --elevated background

title: Create PanelHeader custom component
description: Build a PanelHeader component used atop sidebar panels and bottom panels. Fixed 36px height, --surface background, bottom --border. Contains title text (--text-sm semibold), optional icon, and right-aligned action buttons (close, collapse, more). Matches VS Code panel header pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: PanelHeader renders at 36px height with title and optional actions
Close button fires onClose callback
Collapse button toggles panel open/closed state
Styled consistently across all panels (sidebar, bottom, properties)

title: Create TimelineScrubber custom component
description: Build a TimelineScrubber for simulation playback — a horizontal timeline bar with a draggable playhead, tick marks, and time labels. Shows current simulation tick, total ticks, and chaos event markers as colored dots on the timeline. Uses --primary for playhead, --state-error for chaos markers.
acceptanceCriteria: Timeline renders with draggable playhead indicator
Tick marks shown at regular intervals with time labels
Chaos event markers displayed as colored dots at correct positions
Dragging playhead scrubs to that simulation tick

title: Create SpeedControl custom component
description: Build a SpeedControl dropdown/button group for simulation playback speed. Options: 0.25x, 0.5x, 1x, 2x, 4x, 8x. Displays current speed with a compact label. Clicking cycles through speeds; dropdown shows all options. Uses --primary accent for current speed indicator.
acceptanceCriteria: SpeedControl displays current speed (e.g., '1x')
Click cycles to next speed level
Dropdown shows all speed options with checkmark on current
Speed change updates simulation-store playback rate

title: Create MetricCard custom component
description: Build a MetricCard for displaying simulation metrics (throughput, latency, error rate, etc.) in the bottom panel. Shows: metric label, current value with unit, sparkline mini-chart, and delta indicator (up/down arrow with green/red color). Compact design fitting within a grid of 4-6 cards.
acceptanceCriteria: MetricCard displays label, value, unit, and delta arrow
Delta uses --state-success (green up) or --state-error (red down)
Sparkline renders last 20 data points as tiny line chart
Card fits in a responsive grid alongside other MetricCards

title: Create TabBar custom component
description: Build a TabBar component for the bottom panel and properties panel. Horizontal tab strip with underline indicator, --primary color for active tab, --foreground-muted for inactive. Supports closeable tabs, overflow scrolling when too many tabs, and drag-to-reorder.
acceptanceCriteria: TabBar renders horizontal tabs with underline active indicator
Active tab uses --primary underline and --foreground text
Overflow tabs scroll horizontally with fade gradient
Optional close button on individual tabs

title: Create FloatingToolbar custom component
description: Build a FloatingToolbar that hovers above the canvas. Contains ToolbarButtons for: undo, redo, zoom in, zoom out, fit view, toggle minimap, toggle grid, auto-layout. Positioned top-center of canvas area with --surface bg, --border, and --shadow-md elevation.
acceptanceCriteria: Toolbar floats above canvas, horizontally centered
All 8 buttons render with correct icons and tooltips
Undo/redo buttons disable when canUndo/canRedo is false
Toolbar uses --surface bg with --shadow-md elevation

title: Create KeyboardShortcutBadge custom component
description: Build a KeyboardShortcutBadge component that renders keyboard shortcuts as styled key badges (e.g., [Cmd] + [K]). Auto-detects Mac vs Windows and shows appropriate modifier (Cmd vs Ctrl). Used in command palette items, tooltips, and menu items.
acceptanceCriteria: Badge renders individual keys with --elevated bg and --border
Automatically shows Cmd on Mac and Ctrl on Windows/Linux
Supports modifier combinations (Cmd+Shift+Z)
Compact enough to fit inline in menu items and tooltips

title: Create SimulationTransport custom component
description: Build a SimulationTransport control bar combining play/pause/stop buttons, TimelineScrubber, SpeedControl, and simulation status badge into a unified bottom-panel transport strip. Mimics media player transport controls. Play toggles to pause when running. Stop resets to beginning.
acceptanceCriteria: Transport bar renders play/pause, stop, timeline, and speed in one row
Play button dispatches START_SIMULATION command via command bus
Stop button dispatches STOP_SIMULATION command
Status badge shows idle/running/paused with semantic colors

title: CRITICAL: Fix stale closure in DesignCanvas onConnect callback
description: DesignCanvas.tsx onConnect callback captures edges from the outer scope via closure, causing stale state when multiple connections are made in sequence. The second connection overwrites the first because the callback still references the old edges array. Fix by using a functional updater pattern: setEdges(prev => [...prev, newEdge]) or reading from store.getState() inside the callback.
acceptanceCriteria: Rapidly creating 3+ connections in sequence retains all connections
onConnect uses functional updater or getState() pattern
No stale closure references in any canvas callback
Verified with React strict mode (double render)

title: CRITICAL: Add error boundary around onDrop JSON.parse
description: DesignCanvas.tsx onDrop handler calls JSON.parse(dataTransfer.getData('application/json')) without try/catch. If any non-JSON content is dropped on the canvas (text, images, files from OS), this throws an uncaught exception that crashes the entire canvas. Wrap in try/catch with a user-friendly toast error, and validate the parsed shape with a type guard.
acceptanceCriteria: Dropping non-JSON content shows error toast instead of crashing
Parsed JSON validated against expected shape before use
Invalid JSON shape (e.g., missing type/label) shows specific error message
Canvas remains functional after a failed drop

title: Fix module-scoped nodeIdCounter using crypto.randomUUID
description: DesignCanvas.tsx uses a module-scoped let nodeIdCounter = 0 to generate node IDs. This resets on hot reload (causing duplicate IDs), is not unique across sessions, and creates predictable/colliding IDs. Replace with crypto.randomUUID() which generates unique IDs without any counter state.
acceptanceCriteria: Node IDs use crypto.randomUUID() format
No module-scoped mutable counter exists
IDs are unique across hot reloads and sessions
Existing nodes with old-format IDs still work (backward compatible)

title: Fix DataFlowEdge injecting inline style per instance
description: DataFlowEdge.tsx includes a <style> tag in its render output, injecting CSS rules for every edge instance on the canvas. With 50 edges, this creates 50 duplicate style tags in the DOM. Move the edge animation CSS to globals.css or a dedicated edge styles file that is loaded once.
acceptanceCriteria: No <style> tags rendered by DataFlowEdge component
Edge animation CSS lives in globals.css under React Flow overrides section
Visual appearance of animated edges unchanged
DOM inspector shows zero duplicate style injections

title: Fix ComponentPalette importing entire lucide-react bundle
description: ComponentPalette.tsx imports icons from lucide-react in a way that pulls the entire icon library into the bundle. Create an icon registry (src/lib/icons.ts) that re-exports only the needed icons by name. This reduces the ComponentPalette chunk size significantly since lucide-react contains 1000+ icons.
acceptanceCriteria: Icon registry exports only icons used in the app (~30-40 icons)
ComponentPalette imports from icon registry, not lucide-react directly
Bundle size for ComponentPalette chunk reduced (verify with next build)
No visual change — all palette icons render correctly

title: Fix sorting algorithms crash on empty array input
description: All sorting algorithm implementations (bubble-sort.ts, merge-sort.ts, quick-sort.ts, heap-sort.ts, insertion-sort.ts, selection-sort.ts) crash or produce incorrect results when called with an empty array. Add guard clauses at the top of each generator function to yield an immediate completion step for empty input.
acceptanceCriteria: All 6 sorting algorithms handle empty array without error
All 6 handle single-element array (already sorted) without error
Generator yields a valid final step with empty/single-element input
ArrayVisualizer displays empty state message for empty array

title: Fix 8 no-op command palette actions for Add Component commands
description: The command palette lists 'Add Web Server', 'Add Load Balancer', 'Add Database', etc. but clicking them does nothing — the action callbacks are empty or log to console. Wire each action to create the corresponding node at the canvas center position via canvas-store.addNode() or commandBus.dispatch().
acceptanceCriteria: All 8 'Add Component' commands create the correct node type on canvas
Nodes placed at current viewport center (not 0,0)
Undo snapshot created after each add operation
Command palette closes after action execution

title: Fix unbounded undo history memory in zundo
description: Current zundo setup on canvas-store has no partialize (stores entire state including derived values) and no debounce (every onNodesChange creates an entry). Configure partialize to only store nodes and edges, and add appropriate limits. Note: This is a stopgap fix until FND-032 (custom UndoManager) is implemented.
acceptanceCriteria: zundo partialize only captures nodes and edges (not viewport, selection)
History limit set to 50 entries as interim cap
Drag operations debounced to reduce entry count
Memory usage for undo stays under 5MB for typical diagrams

title: Fix chaos-engine module-level mutable counter
description: chaos-engine.ts uses a module-level mutable variable (let chaosEventId = 0) for generating chaos event IDs. This resets on hot reload and is not safe across sessions. Replace with crypto.randomUUID() or a timestamp-based ID generator.
acceptanceCriteria: Chaos event IDs use crypto.randomUUID() or similar unique generator
No module-level mutable counter exists in chaos-engine.ts
IDs are unique across hot reloads
Existing chaos event handling logic unchanged

title: Fix export-dialog setTimeout memory leak
description: export-dialog.tsx uses setTimeout for copy-to-clipboard feedback ('Copied!' message) but does not clear the timeout when the dialog closes. If the user opens the dialog, clicks copy, then immediately closes, the timeout fires on an unmounted component causing a React warning and potential memory leak. Use useEffect cleanup or useRef to track and clear the timeout.
acceptanceCriteria: setTimeout cleared when export dialog unmounts
No React warning about state update on unmounted component
Copy feedback still shows 'Copied!' for 2 seconds when dialog stays open
useEffect cleanup or useRef pattern used for timeout management

title: Fix PropertiesPanel unsafe type assertions
description: PropertiesPanel.tsx uses unsafe `as` type assertions to cast node.data to specific shapes without runtime validation. If a node has unexpected data (e.g., from a corrupted save or old schema), the assertions succeed at compile time but crash at runtime. Add a type guard function isArchitexNodeData() that validates the shape before accessing properties.
acceptanceCriteria: isArchitexNodeData() type guard validates data shape at runtime
PropertiesPanel shows fallback UI for nodes with invalid data
No uncaught TypeError when selecting a node with missing data fields
Type guard checks all required fields: label, category, componentType, state

title: Fix SystemDesignContent called as function instead of component
description: In the workspace layout, SystemDesignContent is called as a function (SystemDesignContent()) instead of rendered as a JSX component (<SystemDesignContent />). This bypasses React's component lifecycle — hooks inside it run in the parent's context, useEffect cleanups don't work correctly, and error boundaries cannot catch errors from it. Convert to proper JSX component rendering.
acceptanceCriteria: SystemDesignContent rendered as <SystemDesignContent /> JSX
Hooks inside SystemDesignContent run in their own component context
Error boundary can catch errors from SystemDesignContent
No functional change in rendered output

title: Fix all module hooks running unconditionally on every render
description: page.tsx (or workspace-layout.tsx) calls useAlgorithmModule(), useDistributedModule(), useNetworkingModule(), useOSModule(), useConcurrencyModule(), useInterviewModule(), and more on every render — even when only the system-design module is active. This wastes CPU, creates unnecessary React state, and runs intervals (interview timer) when not needed. Guard with early returns or replace with ModuleRenderer (see FND-044).
acceptanceCriteria: Only the active module's hook runs (or no hooks if using ModuleRenderer)
Switching to a module activates only that module's state/effects
Interview timer only runs when interview module is active
Measured re-render count reduced for non-active modules to zero

title: Fix theme system inconsistency between next-themes and store
description: ThemeProvider uses next-themes with enableSystem={true}, which supports a 'system' option. But ui-store stores theme as 'light' | 'dark' only, without a 'system' variant. This causes inconsistency when the user selects system theme — next-themes applies it but the store doesn't reflect it. Align the store's theme type to include 'system' and ensure both sources of truth agree.
acceptanceCriteria: ui-store theme type includes 'system' as a valid option
Selecting 'system' theme persists correctly across reloads
Theme toggle cycles: dark -> light -> system -> dark
next-themes resolvedTheme used for conditional styling

title: Fix ArrayVisualizer using array index as React key
description: ArrayVisualizer.tsx renders sorting array elements using the array index as the React key (key={index}). When elements swap positions during sorting, React reuses the DOM node at each index instead of moving it, breaking CSS transitions and animations. Assign a stable unique ID to each element at creation time and use that as the key.
acceptanceCriteria: Each array element has a stable unique ID assigned at creation
React key uses element ID, not array index
Swap animations visually move elements instead of re-coloring in place
Sorting visualization correctly shows elements moving to new positions

title: Define shared TypeScript types for Web Worker messages
description: Create src/lib/workers/types.ts with typed message interfaces for all worker communication. Define WorkerRequest<T> and WorkerResponse<T> generic wrappers with discriminated union for each worker type (simulation, algorithm, layout). Include error response type, progress reporting type, and cancellation token type.
acceptanceCriteria: WorkerRequest and WorkerResponse generics exported
Discriminated unions for simulation, algorithm, and layout workers
Error response includes code, message, and optional stack
Types importable from both main thread and worker contexts

title: Create simulation Web Worker for off-main-thread tick processing
description: Move simulation tick loop (traffic generation, metric calculation, queuing model updates) to a dedicated Web Worker in src/lib/workers/simulation.worker.ts. Worker receives tick commands and traffic config, runs simulation math, and posts back metric snapshots. Main thread updates stores from worker messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Simulation worker runs tick loop without blocking main thread
Worker receives START, STOP, CONFIGURE, TICK messages
Worker posts back METRICS_UPDATE messages per tick
Canvas remains responsive during high-speed simulation

title: Create algorithm Web Worker for off-main-thread step computation
description: Move sorting/search algorithm step generation to a Web Worker in src/lib/workers/algorithm.worker.ts. Worker receives the array and algorithm choice, pre-computes all steps, and sends them back as an array. This allows smooth scrubbing through algorithm steps without computation jank.
acceptanceCriteria: Algorithm worker pre-computes all steps for a given algorithm
Worker supports all 6 sorting algorithms
Worker posts progress updates during computation
Main thread receives completed step array for playback

title: Create layout Web Worker for auto-layout computation
description: Create a Web Worker in src/lib/workers/layout.worker.ts that runs dagre/elkjs layout algorithms off the main thread. Worker receives node positions and edges, computes new layout, and returns updated positions. Prevents UI freeze when running layout on large diagrams (100+ nodes).
acceptanceCriteria: Layout worker accepts nodes, edges, and layout algorithm choice
Worker returns new node positions after layout computation
Layout computation does not block canvas interaction
Supports hierarchical, force-directed, circular, and grid algorithms

title: Set up Comlink bridge for typed worker communication
description: Install comlink package and create typed wrappers in src/lib/workers/bridge.ts. Comlink allows calling worker functions as if they were local async functions, with full TypeScript typing. Create proxy wrappers for simulation, algorithm, and layout workers.
acceptanceCriteria: Comlink proxies created for all 3 worker types
Worker functions callable with await from main thread
Full TypeScript type safety on both sides of the bridge
Workers terminate cleanly on page unload

title: Configure next.config.ts webpack for Web Worker loading
description: Add webpack configuration in next.config.ts to support Web Worker imports. Configure worker-loader or the native new Worker(new URL(...)) pattern. Ensure workers are bundled as separate chunks and not included in the main bundle. Handle both development (fast refresh) and production builds.
acceptanceCriteria: Workers load correctly in development with fast refresh
Workers bundled as separate chunks in production build
new Worker(new URL('./worker.ts', import.meta.url)) pattern works
No 'Module not found' errors for worker imports

title: Install and configure dagre/elkjs for auto-layout engine
description: Install dagre (lightweight) and @elk/elkjs (advanced) layout libraries. Create src/lib/layout/engine.ts with a unified interface that accepts layout algorithm choice and delegates to the appropriate library. dagre for hierarchical/tree layouts, elkjs for force-directed and complex layouts.
acceptanceCriteria: dagre and elkjs packages installed
LayoutEngine interface with computeLayout(nodes, edges, algorithm) method
dagre computes hierarchical layout correctly
elkjs computes force-directed layout correctly

title: Implement hierarchical (top-down) auto-layout algorithm
description: Create hierarchical layout in src/lib/layout/hierarchical.ts using dagre. Arranges nodes in layers based on edge direction (sources at top, sinks at bottom). Configurable: rankdir (TB/BT/LR/RL), nodesep (horizontal spacing), ranksep (vertical spacing). Suitable for architecture diagrams showing request flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Hierarchical layout arranges nodes in directed layers
Supports top-to-bottom and left-to-right directions
Configurable node and rank spacing
Edges route without crossing nodes

title: Implement force-directed, circular, and grid layout algorithms
description: Add three additional layout algorithms: force-directed (elkjs stress model for organic arrangement), circular (nodes arranged in a circle for peer-to-peer topologies), and grid (nodes in a regular grid for catalog views). Each in its own file under src/lib/layout/.
acceptanceCriteria: Force-directed layout produces organic, non-overlapping arrangement
Circular layout arranges nodes equidistant on a circle
Grid layout arranges nodes in N-column grid
All three accept same LayoutInput interface as hierarchical

title: Add animated node transition when applying auto-layout
description: When auto-layout computes new positions, animate nodes from current positions to target positions using motion/react. Use spring animation (--motion-spring-smooth) with staggered start for visual appeal. During animation, disable node dragging to prevent conflicts.
acceptanceCriteria: Nodes animate smoothly from old to new positions
Animation uses spring physics with 300ms duration
Nodes stagger slightly (20ms per node) for wave effect
Dragging disabled during layout animation, re-enabled after

title: Add auto-layout toolbar button and keyboard shortcut
description: Add an 'Auto Layout' button to the FloatingToolbar with a dropdown for layout algorithm selection (Hierarchical, Force-Directed, Circular, Grid). Add keyboard shortcut Cmd+Shift+L to apply the last-used layout algorithm. Show a brief toast confirming the layout applied.
acceptanceCriteria: Auto Layout button in FloatingToolbar with dropdown for algorithm choice
Cmd+Shift+L applies last-used layout algorithm
Layout applies to all nodes or only selected nodes (if any selected)
Toast confirms layout applied with algorithm name

title: Implement JSON import with schema validation
description: Create src/lib/import/from-json.ts that parses a JSON string, validates it against the SerializedProject or ArchitexNode[]/ArchitexEdge[] schema using a runtime validator (zod or manual type guards), and converts to canvas-ready data. Handle both full project import and nodes-only import.
acceptanceCriteria: Valid JSON project file imports all nodes, edges, and configuration
Invalid JSON shows specific validation error messages
Partial imports (nodes-only) append to existing canvas
Schema version checked and migrated if needed

title: Implement Draw.io XML parser for diagram import
description: Create src/lib/import/from-drawio.ts that parses .drawio XML format (which is mxGraph XML). Extract cell elements, map mxCell shapes to Architex component types (rectangle -> generic, cylinder -> database, etc.), extract positions and connections, and convert to ArchitexNode[]/ArchitexEdge[].
acceptanceCriteria: .drawio file parses without error
Basic shapes (rectangle, cylinder, diamond) map to Architex node types
Edge connections preserved with correct source/target
Position scaling produces reasonable canvas layout

title: Implement Mermaid text parser for diagram import
description: Create src/lib/import/from-mermaid.ts that parses Mermaid flowchart/graph syntax. Use regex-based parsing for the subset of Mermaid syntax relevant to system design diagrams: node declarations, edge connections with labels, and subgraphs. Convert parsed elements to ArchitexNode[]/ArchitexEdge[].
acceptanceCriteria: Mermaid flowchart syntax (graph TD/LR) parsed correctly
Node labels extracted from Mermaid bracket syntax
Edge connections and labels extracted
Subgraphs mapped to node groups (or flattened)

title: Create format detection utility for automatic import routing
description: Create src/lib/import/detect-format.ts that examines file content and returns the detected format: 'json' (starts with { or [), 'drawio' (contains mxGraphModel XML tag), 'mermaid' (starts with 'graph' or 'flowchart'), 'unknown'. Used by the import dialog to automatically select the correct parser.
acceptanceCriteria: detectFormat(content) returns correct format for JSON, Draw.io, and Mermaid
Detection works on both file content strings and File objects
Unknown formats return 'unknown' without throwing
File extension used as hint when content detection is ambiguous

title: Implement clipboard paste handler for diagram import
description: Add a global paste event listener (Cmd+V) that detects diagram content on the clipboard. If clipboard contains JSON, Mermaid, or Draw.io XML, trigger the import flow. Only active when the canvas is focused (not when typing in inputs/textareas). Shows a confirmation dialog before importing.
acceptanceCriteria: Cmd+V with JSON on clipboard triggers import confirmation dialog
Paste ignored when focus is in input, textarea, or code editor
Format auto-detected from clipboard content
User can cancel import from confirmation dialog

title: Implement file drag-drop handler for diagram import
description: Add a full-window drag-drop zone that activates when a file is dragged over the app. Shows a prominent drop overlay ('Drop file to import'). On drop, reads the file, detects format, and triggers the import flow. Supports .json, .drawio, .mmd, and .txt files.
acceptanceCriteria: Dragging a file over the app shows a drop overlay
Dropping a .json file triggers JSON import
Dropping a .drawio file triggers Draw.io import
Invalid file types show error toast

title: Build import dialog UI with format selection and preview
description: Create an ImportDialog component with: file picker button, paste-from-clipboard button, format auto-detection display, preview of detected nodes/edges, import mode toggle (replace vs append), and Import/Cancel buttons. Uses Dialog and ScrollArea components.
acceptanceCriteria: ImportDialog opens from File menu or Cmd+I shortcut
File picker accepts .json, .drawio, .mmd file types
Preview shows detected node/edge count before importing
Replace vs Append mode toggle available

title: Define diagram versioning event types
description: Create src/lib/versioning/types.ts with event types for diagram versioning: NodeAdded, NodeRemoved, NodeMoved, NodeConfigChanged, EdgeAdded, EdgeRemoved, TemplateLoaded, ChaosInjected, ChaosRemoved. Each event includes timestamp, authorId, and a reversible diff (old/new values).
acceptanceCriteria: All diagram mutation events have typed interfaces
Each event includes timestamp, authorId, and event-specific payload
Events are serializable (no functions or class instances)
Diff payloads include both old and new values for reversibility

title: Implement event store for diagram version history
description: Create src/lib/versioning/event-store.ts that stores diagram events in an append-only log. Events are persisted to IndexedDB. Supports appending events, replaying from a point, and querying events by time range. The event store is the source of truth for version history.
acceptanceCriteria: Events append to IndexedDB in chronological order
Replay from any event reconstructs diagram state at that point
Query by time range returns matching events
Event store supports up to 10,000 events per project

title: Implement periodic snapshots for fast version recovery
description: Create a snapshot strategy that captures full diagram state every N events (default: 50). Snapshots stored in IndexedDB alongside events. To reconstruct state at event #237: load snapshot at event #200, then replay events #201-#237. This avoids replaying the entire event log from the beginning.
acceptanceCriteria: Snapshots auto-created every 50 events
State recovery uses nearest snapshot + event replay
Recovery time is O(events since last snapshot), not O(total events)
Snapshots stored in separate IndexedDB store with event index

title: Build version history panel UI
description: Create a HistoryPanel component that displays the diagram's version timeline. Shows event entries with icons, labels, timestamps, and author. Clicking an entry previews the diagram at that point in time (read-only). 'Restore' button reverts to that version. Rendered in a sidebar panel or bottom panel tab.
acceptanceCriteria: History panel lists events in reverse chronological order
Each entry shows icon, label, and relative timestamp
Clicking entry previews diagram state at that point
Restore button creates a new event that sets state to the historical version

title: Build version diff view for comparing diagram states
description: Create a DiffView component that visually compares two diagram versions. Added nodes shown with green border, removed nodes with red border, moved nodes with yellow border, unchanged nodes dimmed. Edge changes highlighted similarly. Side-by-side or overlay mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Diff view highlights added, removed, and modified nodes/edges
Added items shown with --state-success border
Removed items shown with --state-error border
Modified items shown with --state-warning border

title: Implement named branches for diagram version experiments
description: Add branching support to the version system. Users can create named branches ('experiment/chaos-test', 'alternative/microservices') that fork from the current version. Branches maintain independent event logs. Merge back by replacing main with branch state.
acceptanceCriteria: Create branch from any version point with custom name
Branch maintains independent event history from fork point
Switch between branches preserves each branch's state
Merge branch replaces main state (no automatic merge resolution)

title: Create security headers middleware with Content-Security-Policy
description: Create or extend src/middleware.ts to set security headers on all responses. Include Content-Security-Policy with nonce-based script-src (generated per-request), X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin, Permissions-Policy disabling camera/microphone/geolocation.
acceptanceCriteria: CSP header set with nonce-based script-src on every response
Nonce propagated to Next.js <Script> tags via metadata
X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy headers set
Permissions-Policy disables unused browser features

title: Implement CSP nonce generation and propagation
description: Generate a unique nonce per request in middleware.ts using crypto.randomBytes(16).toString('base64'). Pass nonce to layout.tsx via headers. Add nonce attribute to all inline scripts and script-src CSP directive. Ensure React Flow, Sentry, PostHog, and Clerk inline scripts receive the nonce.
acceptanceCriteria: Unique nonce generated per request (not reused across requests)
Nonce appears in CSP script-src directive
All inline <script> tags include nonce attribute
Third-party scripts (Clerk, PostHog, Sentry) work with CSP

title: Configure CORS headers for API routes
description: Add CORS configuration to API route handlers. Allow requests only from the app's own origin in production. In development, allow localhost origins. Set appropriate Access-Control-Allow-Methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE), Access-Control-Allow-Headers (Content-Type, Authorization), and Access-Control-Max-Age (86400).
acceptanceCriteria: API routes return correct CORS headers
Production restricts origin to app domain only
Development allows localhost:3000 and localhost:3001
Preflight OPTIONS requests handled with 204 response

title: Implement rate limiting for API routes
description: Create a rate limiter utility in src/lib/api/rate-limit.ts using an in-memory sliding window counter (for serverless, use Vercel KV or Upstash Redis). Default limits: 100 requests per minute per IP for general API, 10 per minute for auth-related endpoints. Return 429 Too Many Requests with Retry-After header when exceeded.
acceptanceCriteria: Rate limiter tracks requests per IP per time window
429 response returned with Retry-After header on limit exceeded
Different limits configurable per route
Works in both serverless (stateless) and long-running server modes

title: Create .env.example with all required environment variables
description: Create a comprehensive .env.example documenting every environment variable the app needs. Group by service: Clerk (NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, CLERK_SECRET_KEY, URLs), Neon (DATABASE_URL), Inngest (INNGEST_SIGNING_KEY, INNGEST_EVENT_KEY), Resend (RESEND_API_KEY), PostHog (NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY), Sentry (SENTRY_DSN, SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN), and app config (NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL).
acceptanceCriteria: .env.example lists every required environment variable
Variables grouped by service with comment headers
Placeholder values show expected format (e.g., 'pk_test_...')
No actual secrets in the file (verified)

title: Add comprehensive security headers test suite
description: Create tests that verify all security headers are correctly set on responses. Test CSP header contains nonce, X-Frame-Options is DENY, CORS headers are restricted in production. Use fetch against the running app or mock middleware to validate header values.
acceptanceCriteria: Test verifies CSP header is present and contains script-src with nonce
Test verifies X-Frame-Options: DENY
Test verifies X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
Tests pass in CI pipeline
